query: prevalence of MIH according to Weerheijm

retrieved_docs.title: Thunderclap Headache -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: A systematic literature review reported poor population-based data to accurately identify the true incidence and prevalence of thunderclap headaches. [6] The authors determined that the only estimate of a population-based incidence is from the study of a subset of a Swedish population. The incidence is reportedly 43 per 100,000 inhabitants older than 18 years annually. [7] A population-based study in Italy showed a 0.3% lifetime prevalence of TCH in men and women aged 55 to 94. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Familial Combined Hyperlipidemia -- Complications -- Myocardial Infarction
retrieved_docs.content: Familial combined hyperlipidemia ranks high among the common causes of myocardial infarction in young people. In a study by Wiesbauer et al, the prevalence of FCH was 38% among patients younger than 40. [40]

retrieved_docs.title: Meibomian Gland Disease -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: MGD is a globally prevalent disorder. In a meta-analysis by Hassanzadeh et al, the estimated global prevalence is 35.8%, with a higher incidence in men than women. [13] Asian patients have a higher incidence of MGD and meibomian gland dropout than White patients. [13]

retrieved_docs.title: Myocardial Infarction -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: The most common cause of death and disability in the western world and worldwide is coronary artery disease. [11] Based on 2015 mortality data from the National Health Interview Survey (NHIS-CDC), MI mortality was 114,023, and MI any-mention mortality (i.e., MI is mentioned as a contributing factor in the death certificate) was 151,863.

retrieved_docs.title: Idiopathic Pulmonary Hemorrhage -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: IPH is a rare clinical condition that affects mainly the pediatric age group, especially in children aged below 10 years. 80% of cases are thought to occur in this age group. [1] [5] [7] It has a bimodal age distribution with two peaks, one in patients below 5 years and the other in patients above 11 years. [4] Adult-onset IPH usually occurs before age 30. [1] Some cases have been reported in infants, but this is not common. [2] In a 10-year retrospective study conducted in Boston, only 4 out of 154 infants presenting with pulmonary hemorrhage were diagnosed with AIPHI. [2] The incidence of IPH is about 0.24 to 1.23 cases per million globally. [4] [5] There is no sex predilection for the condition in children, but it is reported to have a higher prevalence in adult men than women. [1] [4] There is no known ethnic preference. Some cases of family clustering have been reported, however, no genetic basis for the disease is known so far. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Anterior Myocardial Infarction -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: According to a study, it was found that the incidence of anterior ST-elevation MI (STEMI) accounts for approximately 33% of all STEMIs. [13] Additionally, the incidence of cardiogenic shock in AMI ranges from 5% to 15%. [14]

retrieved_docs.title: Waardenburg Syndrome -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: The prevalence of Waardenburg syndrome varies geographically. In the Netherlands, the estimated incidence is approximately 1 in 212,000 individuals, with low penetrance observed in about 20% of cases. Globally, the estimated prevalence is approximately 1 in 42,000 individuals. Types 1 and 2 of Waardenburg syndrome are the most frequently reported subtypes worldwide, whereas type 3 remains exceedingly rare. [10] Type 4 accounts for approximately 19% of all reported cases. [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Idiopathic Pulmonary Hemosiderosis -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: Due to the rare nature of idiopathic pulmonary hemosiderosis (IPH), the incidence and prevalence of the disease are relatively unknown. Many patients previously reported to have IPH are likely misdiagnosed and have a diffuse alveolar hemorrhage. A Swedish study from 1984 estimated an incidence of 0.24 per million children per year, using data collected from 1950-1979. [2] A retrospective study from Japan estimated approximately 1.23 cases per million per year. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Hollenhorst Plaque -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: In the Beaver Dam Eye Study, comprising almost 5000 patients, the prevalence of HPs was 1.3%, and the 5-year incidence was 0.9%. [9] In a study of 130 consecutive patients with a diagnosis of HP alongside central or branch RAO, the mean age of the patients was 68 ± 16 years, and the incidence of symptomatic patients was 61%. [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Corneal Dystrophy -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: Demographically, CD has a variety of presentations. Both genders are equally affected by CD, although it has been shown that Fuchs CD is more common in females. [28] [30] FECD is more prevalent in patients of Caucasian descent. [24] CD can be more prevalent based on global regions, such as GCD type II being predominantly European, GDLD common in Japan, Meesmann CD is found in families with German and Danish descent, and LCD type II being found in patients of Finnish descent. [4] [31] [32] Some dystrophies present as early as birth, such as Fleck CD, CHED, and CSCD, while others develop later on in life, typically in the second or third decade of life, such as late-onset FECD and LCD type II. [1] [7] [4] [24]

retrieved_docs.title: Idiopathic Pulmonary Hemorrhage -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: The prognosis of IPH depends on factors such as the time of diagnosis, how early treatment is commenced, and the presence of co-morbidities. [4] [7] Prompt diagnosis and early treatment lead to a better prognosis. [4] [11] It has an average survival rate of 2.5 years, although one study reported 86% of patients surviving beyond 5 years. [7] Its mortality rate is 50%, however, no mortality was recorded in the 4 infants with AIPHI in a retrospective study. [2] [4] [11] Generally, the disease runs a more severe course with a worse outcome in children and adolescents compared to adults. [1] Mortality is usually caused by acute respiratory failure following massive alveolar hemorrhage or complications of chronic respiratory failure and cor pulmonale following severe pulmonary fibrosis. [1] Long-term steroid use reduces morbidity and mortality. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Phakic Intraocular Lens Myopia -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: There is a huge variation in the prevalence of myopia in different regions and ethnic groups. [11] The prevalence of myopia can be estimated to be around 70% to 90% in Asia, around 30% to 40% in Europe and America, and 10% to 20% in Africa. [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Congenital Myotonic Dystrophy -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: Myotonic dystrophy is the most prevalent adult muscular dystrophy among the people of European ancestry with a minimum prevalence of 11.84/100,000 [13] [14] The incidence of CMD is 1 in 47619 live births, higher in specific areas (Quebec, Canada) [4] Spain - 0.08 per 10,000 live births [15] Canada - 2.1 in 100,000 (1 in 47,619) live births [16] South Africa - 3 per 100,000 live births [17] Taiwan 0.46/100,000 inhabitants (adult type) [18] In a systemic review, the prevalence of all muscular dystrophies was 19.8 to 25.1 per 100,000 person-years. Myotonic dystrophy (0.5 to 18.1 per100 000), Duchenne muscular dystrophy (1.7 to 4.2), and facioscapulohumeral muscular dystrophy (3.2 to 4.6 in 100 000) were the most common types. [19]

retrieved_docs.title: Nipah Virus -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: In Bangladesh, the epidemiology of NiV differed, starting with an outbreak of encephalitis in Meherpur in 2001. The primary difference included the absence of pigs being involved in the outbreak, as pig farming was not being practiced in Bangladesh, which is a predominantly Muslim country. Following this, multiple seasonal outbreaks have occurred, each of which is thought to be separate spillover events. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Periventricular and Intraventricular Hemorrhage -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: The worldwide incidence of PIVH ranges from 3.70 to 44.68%. [2] A recent study showed an overall incidence of 36.2%, with severe grades (III, IV) being present in 7.1% of them. [9] The overall frequency of PIVH grades I, II, III, and IV in preterm infants is 17.0%, 12.1%, 3.3%, and 3.8%, respectively. [9] When PIVH occurs, about 50% occur on the first day of life, and by the third day of life, it is 90%. [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Pseudohypoparathyroidism -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: Pseudohypoparathyroidism and Albright hereditary osteodystrophy are very rare disorders. The estimated prevalence is between 0.3 and 1.1 cases per 100000 population, depending on geographic location. [1] [8]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 4 -- Community 2885
retrieved_docs.content: COMPLICATIONS MAINLY RELATED TO PREGNANCY is classified as a subcategory within the broader group of Complications of pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium, indicating that these health issues are primarily associated with the gestational period and its immediate aftermath.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2304
retrieved_docs.content: Early, first or unspecified trimester hemorrhage is classified as spotting complicating pregnancy, unspecified trimester.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1494
retrieved_docs.content: A gestational age of 29‑30 weeks completed is associated with a liveborn infant, indicating that pregnancies reaching this stage can result in a live birth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 4 -- Community 2811
retrieved_docs.content: Other complications of pregnancy are clinically associated with إنهاء الحمل – اختياري (optional termination of pregnancy) and also have a relationship with اضطراب رحمي (uterine disorder). This indicates that uterine disorders are considered among the other complications of pregnancy and that optional termination of pregnancy can be clinically linked to these pregnancy‑related complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 3 -- Community 1701
retrieved_docs.content: Respiratory failure; insufficiency; arrest (adult) is a specific adult respiratory condition that is classified under the broader category of اضطراب تَنَفُّسِيّ (respiratory disorder).

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 4 -- Community 2911
retrieved_docs.content: Allowed qualifier is recognized as a complication of the esophageal disorder اضطراب المريء.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 4 -- Community 2770
retrieved_docs.content: Acute myocardial infarction is classified as encompassing the subgroup of thrombotic and arterial embolism cases (حالات الخثار والانصمام الشريانية, SMQ), indicating that these thrombotic and embolic arterial events are considered members of the acute myocardial infarction category. This classification aids in the systematic organization of cardiovascular diagnoses and informs clinical assessment and management strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2921
retrieved_docs.content: Esophageal disorders are classified as اضطرابات مَريئِيّة أخرى.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 4 -- Community 2914
retrieved_docs.content: Esophageal disorders are classified under the category “اضطرابات مَريئِيّة أخرى” and are clinically associated with “تعاطي الكحول” (alcohol consumption), indicating that alcohol use is a relevant factor in the development or exacerbation of these esophageal conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2106
retrieved_docs.content: COMPLICATIONS MAINLY RELATED TO PREGNANCY is categorized as a specific type of maternal condition that can complicate pregnancy, childbirth, or the puerperium, and it is considered a subcategory under the broader classification of other conditions or status of the mother complicating these reproductive processes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 33 -- Level 1 -- Community 109
retrieved_docs.content: disorders, and obstetric interventions together form a comprehensive framework for risk assessment, care planning, and management of maternal‑fetal outcomes during pregnancy, delivery, and the postpartum period.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 1 -- Community 61
retrieved_docs.content: and related adverse events to specific drugs, contraindications and higher‑order classification components, providing clinicians with a structured reference for diagnosis, risk assessment and therapeutic decision‑making.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 2720
retrieved_docs.content: The concept “výsledek těhotenství” (outcome of pregnancy) is classified as a specific type of “Finding related to pregnancy,” indicating that pregnancy outcomes are considered pregnancy‑related findings.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 1632
retrieved_docs.content: COMPLICATIONS MAINLY RELATED TO PREGNANCY are classified as having the parent conditions of hemorrhage during pregnancy, abruptio placenta, and placenta previa, indicating that these specific obstetric hemorrhagic events constitute the primary pregnancy‑related complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 27 -- Level 3 -- Community 1596
retrieved_docs.content: Acute and unspecified renal failure, which is classified as فشل كلوي حاد, is clinically associated with الحماض (acidosis) and فرطُ بوتاسيوم الدَّم (hyperkalemia). The presence of الحماض and فرطُ بوتاسيوم الدَّم frequently co‑occurs, reflecting their linked pathophysiology in renal impairment. Moreover, acute and unspecified renal failure is considered related to, and possibly synonymous with, وكلاء الكليون السفلي (lower urinary tract agents), indicating an overlap in terminology or clinical context between these entities.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 1 -- Community 110
retrieved_docs.content: (التخثر الوريدي) is identified as a subtype of venous disorder (اضطراب الوريد). This integrated knowledge connects drug classes, contraindications, and therapeutic strategies for shock with the pathophysiology of arterial and venous thromboembolic conditions.
